statement: All 29 studies showed safety of intermittent fasting and absence of adverse effects
direct_quote: All studies showed the safety of intermittent fasting and absence of adverse effects
source_title: The effect of intermittent fasting on mortality in patients with type 2 diabetes and metabolic disease with high cardiovascular risk: A systematic review

statement: Restrictive dietary strategies including intermittent fasting may offer metabolic and performance-related benefits
direct_quote: these approaches may offer metabolic and performance-related benefits
source_title: Dietary Supplements and Popular Nutritional Strategies among Young Adults: Effectiveness, Safety, and Potential Health Risks–A Current Review of the Literature

statement: Common IF protocols include fasting 16-20 hours for 1-3 days/week, 24-hour fasts twice weekly, or alternate-day fasting with 75% energy restriction
direct_quote:
fasting for 16 to 20 hours of a 24 -hour day for 1 -3 days a week, fasting for 24 -hours two 

times a week, or alternate -day fasting
source_title: Intermittent Fasting (IF) Promotes Longevity through Alterations of the Mammalian Target of Rapamycin (mTOR) and the Epigenome

statement: Intermittent fasting is not superior to regular dieting; both produce equivalent weight loss and similar changes in blood pressure, cholesterol, and inflammation
direct_quote: intermittent fasting is not better than regular dieting; both produce the same amount of weight loss and similar changes in blood pressure, cholesterol and inflammation
source_title: Research review shows intermittent fasting works for weight loss, health changes
